patch.value: Lock contention auto-retry: bounded jittered wait before lock_conflict so parallel agent mutations self-heal

Parallel mutations on the SAME item hard-fail immediately: acquireLock (src/core/lock/lock.ts) throws lock_conflict (exit 4) the moment a live lock exists — no wait, no retry. Empirical: 12 parallel `pm update` on one item → 5 succeed, 7 lock_conflict, even though each lock is held only for milliseconds. Agents firing parallel tool calls (or multiple agent sessions/programmatic spawns) must hand-roll retry loops. Fix: bounded jittered retry inside acquireLock before throwing — e.g. settings `locks.wait_ms` (default ~3000, 0 = today's fail-fast) + `PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS` env override + `--lock-wait-ms` flag; poll with jittered backoff (e.g. 25→50→100→200ms) until acquired or budget exhausted. Also add `retry_after_ms` hint to the structured lock_conflict error payload so callers that still time out know to retry. Integrity is already perfect under contention (no corruption, no lock debris, history consistent) — this is purely availability/UX. Note: a single pm-mcp server already serializes via SerialQueue (pm-3puw), so this mainly hits parallel CLI invocations and multiple concurrent MCP/agent sessions on one workspace.

author: claude-code-agent

Agents firing parallel tool calls (or multiple agent sessions/programmatic spawns) still need hand-rolled retry loops. If the wait budget exhausts, include a `retry_after_ms` hint in the structured lock_conflict payload so callers know when to retry. Note: a single pm-mcp server already serializes via SerialQueue (pm-3puw), so this mainly hits parallel CLI invocations and multiple concurrent MCP/agent sessions on one workspace.

bounded jittered lock wait + retry_after_ms recovery hint

patch.value:
## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit: under parallel multi-agent mutation stress, contended item locks fail immediately with lock_conflict, forcing every agent to implement its own retry loop (or lose work). Data integrity held in all stress runs; the gap is ergonomics/self-healing, not corruption.

## Design (implemented, uncommitted as of 2026-07-04)
- `acquireLock` gains a bounded wait loop: exponential backoff 25ms → 200ms with full jitter (0.5x–1.5x), waiting up to a budget before surfacing lock_conflict.
- Budget resolution order: `PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS` env (non-negative int) > `locks.wait_ms` from settings > 0 (fail-fast = exact pre-feature behavior). Default shipped in SETTINGS_DEFAULTS: 3000ms. Config key `locks_wait_ms` (nested-settings descriptor, `pm config set locks_wait_ms <n>`), optional in the settings validator so existing settings.json files stay valid.
- Stale-lock handling folded into the same loop: stale locks are removed (subject to force_required_for_stale_lock governance) with removals capped at MAX_STALE_LOCK_REMOVALS=3 to prevent livelock between competing waiters.
- On exhausted budget, lock_conflict error now carries `recovery.retry_after_ms: 250` (new optional field on PmCliErrorRecoveryPayload) so agents/MCP clients can machine-read when to retry; message appends "after waiting <n>ms".
- `waitMs` threaded through acquireLock call sites including restore and history-rewrite.

## Constraints / decisions
- 0 must remain a valid budget meaning fail-fast (backward compat + tests that assert immediate conflict).
- Jittered sleep uses Math.random by design (production code path; the no-Date/no-random rule applies to workflow scripts, not src).
- Interacts with pm-zwib (stale-lock takeover race): the removal cap bounds the blast radius but does NOT fix the two-waiters-both-remove race — pm-zwib stays open and depends on this item.

## Remaining before close (per AC)
- CONFIGURATION.md docs for locks_wait_ms / PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS.
- contracts:update if any flag surface changed.
- Parallel-mutation stress evidence recorded via pm test.

Before PR: run fast suite + coverage gate, add CONFIGURATION.md docs for locks_wait_ms/PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS, contracts:update if needed, record stress evidence via pm test, then close pm-2muu and pm-8t5x together with structured resolution fields.

message: Shipped and verified: bounded jittered lock wait (locks.wait_ms=3000 default) self-heals parallel-agent lock contention; sandbox stress 12/12 vs pre-fix 5/12.

author: claude-code-agent

patch.value:
## Context
Filed by the 2026-07-03 concurrency audit: under parallel multi-agent mutation stress, contended item locks failed immediately with lock_conflict, forcing every agent to implement its own retry loop. Data integrity held in all stress runs; the gap was ergonomics/self-healing, not corruption.

## Shipped behavior
- `acquireLock` waits with bounded exponential backoff and jitter up to `locks.wait_ms` before surfacing lock_conflict.
- Budget resolution order: `PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS` env override, then `locks.wait_ms` from settings, with `0` preserving fail-fast behavior.
- Exhausted waits include a structured `retry_after_ms` recovery hint for agents and MCP clients.
- Stale-lock cleanup is bounded and serialized behind owner-token cleanup gates to avoid lock-debris and replacement-gate races.
- Relevant lock acquisition call sites thread the configured wait budget through create/copy/profile/schema/reindex/background-refresh/package-import/history/restore paths.

## Evidence
Shipped in commit 010bb349 / PR #430. Sandbox stress on rebuilt dist: 12/12 parallel updates on one item succeeded with all tags landed and zero lock_conflict, versus the pre-fix 5/12 baseline. Verification recorded on this item includes focused lock/config/claim suites, full coverage at 100/100/100/100, lint/static quality, changelog check, and hosted review follow-up evidence.
